rulings.Case Title: RALPH ZUNIGA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Brayton Purcell LLP¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell informs Mr. Zuniga of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 10:00am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK FARLEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Brand Energy Services of California, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and premises owner/contractor liability and taken off calendar as to strict liability. Defendant did not satisfy its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate with its moving papers that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims against defendant. Assuming without deciding that the declarations of Mr. Singletary, Mr. Valentine and Mr. Kuhn are admissible, when read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony they merely create a triable issue whether defendant worked at Shell Oil in Martinez during the relevant time frame. Those declarations do not sustain defendant¿s burden of demonstrating that defendant never performed work at Shell Oil that involved the disturbance of asbestos-containing materials. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his strict liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:00am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
